async_execution.mechanism: Cline hooks run synchronously before/after tool events; no async execution documented

context_injection.mechanism: context_modification_output: Cline hooks can inject additional context into the agent's session via hook output

decision_control.mechanism: pre_tool_use_cancellation: PreToolUse hooks can cancel (block) the tool invocation; no allow or modify sub-capabilities documented

handler_types.mechanism: Cline hooks execute shell commands only; no HTTP, LLM prompt, or agent handler types documented

hook_scopes.mechanism: global_and_project_hooks: Cline supports global (user-wide) and project-level hook configuration

input_modification.mechanism: context_modification_output: Cline PreToolUse hooks can return modified context/input before tool execution

json_io_protocol.mechanism: Cline hooks communicate via exit codes and output text; no structured JSON stdin/stdout protocol documented

matcher_patterns.mechanism: Cline hooks fire on configured lifecycle events; no per-tool pattern matching documented

permission_control.mechanism: Cline hooks can cancel tool invocations (decision_control) but cannot grant/deny tool availability

providerExtensions.name: Eight Named Hook Types
providerExtensions.description: Cline defines 8 hook types covering the full task lifecycle: TaskStart (new task begins), TaskResume (interrupted task resumes), TaskCancel (task cancelled by user), TaskComplete (task succeeds), PreToolUse (before any tool executes), PostToolUse (after tool completes), UserPromptSubmit (user sends a message), PreCompact (before context truncation). Each runs at a specific, documented point in the lifecycle. Hook input JSON uses camelCase field names matching the hook type (e.g., preToolUse, taskStart).

providerExtensions.name: contextModification Output Field for Injecting Conversation Context
providerExtensions.description: Hook stdout JSON supports a contextModification field (string). When set, the text is injected into the active conversation as context for Cline to consider. This allows hooks to supply dynamic information — project-specific context at task start, validation results before a tool runs, environment state mid-task — without requiring the user to manually paste it. The cancel and errorMessage fields control operation blocking.

providerExtensions.name: Global and Project Hook Scopes with Additive Execution
providerExtensions.description: Hooks can be stored globally (~/Documents/Cline/Hooks/) or per-project (.clinerules/hooks/). When both exist for the same hook type, both execute: global hooks run first, then workspace hooks. If either returns cancel: true, the operation stops. Project hooks can be committed to version control.

providerExtensions.name: Platform-Specific Hook File Format
providerExtensions.description: Hook filenames are platform-sensitive: Windows requires <HookName>.ps1 (PowerShell), macOS/Linux requires extensionless <HookName> (executable shell script or binary). Wrong-platform naming is silently ignored by hook discovery. On Windows, hook enable/disable toggling is not yet supported — hooks run whenever the file exists. Toggle support is consistent on macOS/Linux via the Hooks UI.

providerExtensions.name: PreToolUse Can Cancel Tool Execution Before It Happens
providerExtensions.description: PreToolUse hooks receive the tool name and full parameters before execution. Returning cancel: true with an errorMessage blocks the operation entirely and surfaces the message to the user. PostToolUse hooks can also return cancel: true to stop the task, but cannot undo the tool execution that already occurred.

providerExtensions.name: Active Model Provider and Slug in Hook Input
providerExtensions.description: Every hook input JSON includes a model object with provider (machine-stable provider identifier) and slug (model identifier, e.g. "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5"). If unavailable, both fields fall back to the string "unknown". This allows hooks to implement model-aware logic — e.g., stricter guardrails for less capable models or routing to different external systems based on provider.
